cwes.name: Creation of Immutable Text Using String Concatenation

refs.title: String building in loops MUST use builder/join patterns instead of concatenation
String building in loops MUST use builder/join patterns instead of concatenation

cwes.name: Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table

refs.title: Queries on large tables MUST use indexes and MUST NOT perform full table scans
Queries on large tables MUST use indexes and MUST NOT perform full table scans

cwes.name: Excessive Execution of Sequential Searches of Data Resource

refs.title: Repeated lookups MUST use indexed structures (maps, sets) instead of linear search
Repeated lookups MUST use indexed structures (maps, sets) instead of linear search

cwes.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

refs.title: Database queries inside loops are forbidden (N+1 problem)
String concatenation in loops MUST use a builder or join pattern
Hot-path code MUST NOT perform redundant computation; cache or memoize results
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

cwes.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

refs.title: Sequential awaits on independent async calls MUST be parallelised
Synchronous blocking I/O MUST NOT be used in async contexts
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available
CPU computation MUST NOT be unnecessarily inefficient
Regular expressions MUST NOT use catastrophic backtracking patterns
Regular expressions MUST NOT use patterns susceptible to catastrophic backtracking
Unbounded result sets MUST be paginated or limited
Database connections MUST use connection pooling
Large collections MUST use lazy iteration (generators, streams) instead of full materialization
Caches MUST have eviction policies; unbounded caches are forbidden
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts
Operations MUST NOT allow small inputs to trigger disproportionate resource consumption
Operations MUST NOT allow small inputs to trigger disproportionate resource consumption

cwes.name: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

refs.title: Resource allocation MUST be throttled with configurable limits
Resource allocation MUST be throttled with configurable limits

cwes.name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime

refs.title: File and network handles MUST be released promptly after use
Resources MUST be released promptly after use

cwes.name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value

refs.title: Memory allocation MUST NOT use excessively large size values from untrusted input

cwes.name: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop

refs.title: Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible
Resource allocation inside loops MUST be minimised; pre-allocate where possible

cwes.name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component

refs.title: Mutable default arguments MUST NOT be used; create fresh instances per call
Memory-intensive operations MUST use streaming instead of loading entire datasets
Thread/coroutine pools MUST have bounded sizes with configurable limits
Resource consumption MUST be bounded with limits and timeouts
Recursive algorithms MUST have depth limits or use iterative alternatives
Recursive algorithms MUST have depth limits to prevent stack overflow

cwes.name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

refs.title: Batch operations MUST be preferred over individual operations for bulk data
Data access operations MUST be batched; no queries inside loops

cwes.name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

refs.title: Background tasks MUST be offloaded from request-handling threads
Remote resource access MUST use timeouts and async patterns where available
